name: dev-rigor-stack
description: A six-gate delivery discipline for AI coding agents, the skills it invokes, and three always-on hooks: a rigor reflex, a per-task rigor router, and a mechanical grounding check.

skills.name: dev-rigor-stack
skills.role: the stack itself — orchestrates the loop + release gate

skills.name: coder-tdd-qa
skills.role: BUILD gate — test-first engineering + QA standards

skills.name: audit-lite
skills.role: REVIEW lane — fast single-pass audit of a small change

skills.name: audit-team
skills.role: REVIEW lane — multi-role deep audit for high-blast units

skills.name: gauntletgate
skills.role: REVIEW lane + release gate — adversarial stage-gate (lite/walkthrough/full)

plugin.installs_to: ~/.claude/dev-rigor-plugin/ + hook entries in settings.json (SessionStart, SubagentStart, UserPromptSubmit, PostToolUse, Stop)
plugin.requires: Node.js — REQUIRED for the hooks (they are small Node scripts). The six skills install without Node; if Node is missing the installer reports the hooks as skipped. Anyone running a coding agent almost certainly has Node already.

hooks.name: dev-rigor-reflex
hooks.role: Always-on one-page distillation of the discipline — the proof ladder (rigor sized to blast radius), the never-shrink rules, and the evidence receipt. Injected every session and into subagents; delegates the heavy mechanics to the skills above.

hooks.name: dev-rigor-router
hooks.role: Per-task rigor router — classifies each prompt and injects ONLY the matching task protocol from plugin/disciplines/ (investigation for bug work, render/run grounding for UI/artifact work, decomposition + per-story evidence for multi-part work, release discipline for tag work). Each protocol injects at most once per session; no match means silence.

hooks.name: dev-rigor-ground
hooks.role: Mechanical grounding check — records edits to runnable/viewable files and execution-tool calls, then blocks a stop (once per session) if runnable artifacts were edited but nothing was ever executed or rendered. Catches the provable-theater floor; the router's grounding protocol covers the judgment above it.

acknowledgments.name: ponytail
acknowledgments.author: DietrichGebert
acknowledgments.note: Prior art: the reflex hook borrows ponytail's always-on-reflex FORM, aimed at the opposite end (more proof rather than less code). No ponytail code is used, bundled, or required.

acknowledgments.name: fablize
acknowledgments.author: fivetaku
acknowledgments.note: Prior art: fablize's Fable-vs-Opus comparison proved per-task discipline routing and verification grounding transferable to smaller models; the rigor router and grounding check are clean-room implementations of those two concepts. No fablize code was read, used, bundled, or required.
